error.networkError:
Network connection failed. Unable to generate audio.

Possible reasons:
• Unstable network connection
• Firewall blocking the connection
• Azure TTS service temporarily unavailable

Suggestions:
• Check your network connection
• Try again later
error.timeout:
Generation timed out. Please try:
• Shorten the text length
• Check your internet speed
• Try again later
error.longTextTimeout:
Generation timed out (text length: {count} characters).

Processing long texts takes more time. Please try:
• Wait longer (long texts may take 5–10 minutes)
• Generate in segments (no more than 1000 characters at a time)
• Check your internet speed
• Try again later
error.segmentFailed:
Segmentation of long text failed.

Suggestions:
• Ensure a stable network connection
• Try shortening the text length
• Manually merge the audio after generating it in segments
error.exportNetworkError: Network connection failed. Unable to generate audio. Please check your network connection and try again.
error.exportTimeout: Generation timed out. Please try shortening the text or retry later.
error.exportLongTextTimeout: Generation timed out (text length: {count} characters). Processing long texts takes more time. Please wait or try generating in segments.
error.longTextDetected: Long text detected ({count} characters). It will be processed concurrently (approximately {segments} segments). Please wait...

description.zhiwei: Lively and energetic, sunny, cheerful, and full of vitality. Suitable for animation narration, short video explanations, young male characters in novels, and light educational videos.
description.xiaoyu: All-emotional, with a gentle, friendly, and natural voice quality that supports various emotions. Suitable for audiobooks, film and TV commentary, emotional radio programs, customer service robots, and long-text reading.
description.xiaofeng: Professional and steady, with a deep, authoritative, and magnetic voice close to traditional radio news anchors. Suitable for news broadcasting, documentary narration, corporate promotional videos, serious academic lectures, and political commentary.
description.xiaomei: Gentle and sweet, with a crisp, sweet, and polite voice like a professional service representative or a caring older sister. Suitable for online customer service, voice assistants, children's stories, shopping mall announcements, and lifestyle tips.
description.yunjian: Passionate and energetic, with a fast speaking pace, strong power, and a dynamic feel. The excited voice is highly infectious. Suitable for sports event commentary, competitive game commentary, promotional advertisements, and 'roaring' narration in short videos.
description.yunxia: Cute and innocent, with a tender, pure, and adorable childlike voice. Suitable for early childhood education, reading picture books for children, voicing very young characters in cartoons, and dubbing cute pet videos.
description.xiaobei: Bold, humorous, and down-to-earth, with the unique humor and charm of people from Northeast China, making it sound very friendly. Suitable for funny skits, lifestyle vlogs, storytelling set in Northeast China, and highly recognizable livestreaming for product sales.
description.xiaoni: Simple, honest, and flavorful, reflecting the local customs and culture of Northwest China. Full of emotion and strength. Suitable for introducing history and culture (such as tourism in Xi'an), promoting local specialty foods, and narrating films and documentaries about rural life in Northwest China.
description.wanlong: Mature, stable, and business-like, with a typical Hong Kong elite tone and standard, professional pronunciation. Suitable for financial news reporting, corporate promotional videos, and commentary on Hong Kong-style business dramas.
description.hiugaai: Soft, intellectual, and authentic—this is a very standard Cantonese pronunciation with a natural feel. Suitable for news broadcasts, radio hosting, and lifestyle guides in Cantonese-speaking regions.
description.hiumaan: Elegant, composed, and scholarly. Compared to Xiaojia, her voice sounds more like that of a learned and serene speaker. Suitable for audiobook narration, documentary narration, and high-end brand advertising.
description.yunjhe: Gentle, refined, and warm—this 'milk-style' voice embodies the typical Taiwanese male pronunciation. The tone is calm and non-aggressive, making it perfect for healing-style short videos, commentary on youth idol dramas, emotional radio programs, and life tip reminders.
description.hsiaochen: Sweet, sunny, and full of energy—this voice sounds like a cheerful, approachable girl next door. It’s highly likable and ideal for entertainment news, variety show narration, youthful vlogs, and morning alarm clocks.
description.hsiaoyu: Professional, clear, and intellectual—this is the quintessential 'Taiwanese Mandarin' anchor style. With crisp articulation and a very friendly demeanor, it’s suitable for educational videos, online course lectures, smart voice assistants, and formal voice guidance scenarios.
